title: Recent Victories - Farris Law Firm
metaDescription: People v. Tenbrink - DUI reduced to wet reckless, even though incident was live streamed on YouTube People v. Kalfaian - Drug possession case dismissed
contentHtml:
People v. Tenbrink – DUI reduced to wet reckless, even though incident was live streamed on YouTube

People v. Kalfaian – Drug possession case dismissed when we demanded substance testing and the substance was SUGAR!

People v. Zakiyants – complete dismissal of PC 242 battery charge

People v. Jendo – DUI reduced to wet reckless to help client’s immigration status

People v. Pulido – dismissal of case because it was filed after the statute of limitations had run

People v. Mitchell – successful military diversion, case dismissed upon completion of classes

People v. Deangelis – successful seal of arrest

People v. Preciado – DUI with high BAC reduced to wet reckless

People v. Martinez – DUI reduction to wet reckless while client has a commercial license

People v. Buitron – reinstated probation on a 6 year old bench warrant

People v. McGee – serious felony negotiated down from prison time to probation only

People v. Abdilmanova – complete dismissal

People v. Nigro – complete dismissal

People v. Garcia – reduction to public intoxication from a DUI

People v. Franco – diversion only for resisting arrest case, no jail and no probation

People v. Daza – no jail, probation only on serious misdemeanor case

People v. Elger – vandalism case with diversion only, resulting in dismissal

People v. Igartua – serious fighting and assault case reduced to disturbing the peace

People v. Duran – diversion granted on hit and run case

People v. Curtis – reduced DUI to exhibition of speed only

People v. Sanchez – successful early termination of probation after misdemeanor case.

People v. Mier – complete dismissal of hit and run case due to mistaken identity
